PRELIMINARY PROSPECTUS SUBJECT TO COMPLETION, DATED JUNE 10, 2026

MetaOptics Ltd

3,000,000 American Depositary Shares Representing 36,000,000 Ordinary Shares

MetaOptics Ltd, an exempted company incorporated in the Cayman Islands, is offering
3,000,000 American depositary shares ("ADSs") representing our ordinary shares,
par value S$0.00000025 per share. Each ADS represents 12 ordinary shares. This is
our initial public offering in the United States, and no public market currently
exists for our ADSs. Our ordinary shares have been listed on Catalist of the
Singapore Exchange Securities Trading Limited since September 9, 2025 under the
stock code "9MT." We expect the initial public offering price will be between
US$5.00 and US$7.00 per ADS. We have submitted an application to list the ADSs on
the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol "MOT." MetaOptics Ltd is a holding
company that conducts all of its operations through its wholly-owned subsidiaries
in Singapore and the United States. Investing in the ADSs involves risks.

PRICE US$ PER ADS
We have agreed to issue warrants to Roth Capital Partners, LLC and The Benchmark
Company, LLC, as the representatives of the underwriters. Joint Book-Running
Managers Roth Capital Partners Benchmark, a StoneX Company.

PROSPECTUS SUMMARY
Overview
We are a vertically integrated metalens technology company, combining core
competencies across metalens equipment, foundry, products, and metaoptics
artificial intelligence. We conduct our operations through our wholly-owned
subsidiaries in Singapore and the United States. This prospectus contains
information from an industry report commissioned by us and prepared by China
Insights Industry Consultancy Limited.

Summary of Industry
Global revenue grew from about US$2.3 million in 2020 to US$64.5 million in 2025,
representing a CAGR of 94.5%, according to the Independent Market Report.

THE OFFERING
ADSs offered by us 3,000,000 ADSs (or 3,450,000 ADSs if the underwriters exercise
their over-allotment option in full).
Offering price per ADS We currently expect the initial public offering price to
be between US$5.00 and US$7.00 per ADS.
Over-allotment option We have granted to the underwriters a 45-day option to
purchase from us up to an additional 15% of the ADSs sold in this offering.
The ADSs Each ADS represents 12 ordinary shares, par value S$0.00000025 per share.
The ordinary shares We will issue 36,000,000 ordinary shares represented by the
ADSs in this offering.
Use of proceeds We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering primarily to
support our expansion plans in the United States.
Listing We have applied to have the ADSs listed on the Nasdaq Capital Market under
the symbol "MOT."

RISK FACTORS
We have a substantial customer and supplier concentration, and any adverse change
in these relationships could materially and adversely impact our business,
results of operations and financial condition. Haur-Jye Technology, Taiwan,
accounted for 74.6% of our FY2025 revenue. MMI Systems Pte Ltd, Singapore,
accounted for 81.0% of our FY2025 purchases and is a wholly owned subsidiary of
MMI Holdings Limited, one of our principal shareholders. We have registered eight patents in
Taiwan and four patents in the PRC. China customers, PRC patents, and China market
data are discussed in this filing, but the company does not disclose a variable
interest entity, VIE structure, or contractual-control arrangement.

USE OF PROCEEDS
We estimate that we will receive net proceeds of approximately US$14.3 million
from this offering (or US$16.8 million if the underwriters exercise their option
to purchase additional ADSs in full), assuming an initial public offering price of
US$6.00 per ADS, the mid-point of the estimated initial public offering price range
set forth on the front cover of this prospectus, after deducting estimated
underwriting discounts and commissions and estimated offering expenses payable by
us. We intend to use the net proceeds from this offering primarily to support our
expansion plans in the United States, including product development and strategic
partnerships, AI-powered software and algorithmic capabilities, working capital,
and general corporate purposes.

CAPITALIZATION
The following table sets forth our capitalization as of December 31, 2025.
As of December 31, 2025 Actual As adjusted S$ US$ S$ US$
Cash and cash equivalents 8,789,537 6,845,071 27,141,117 21,136,822

Total equity 10,047,760 7,824,944 28,399,349 22,116,702
TOTAL CAPITALIZATION 12,153,907 9,465,158 30,505,496 23,756,916

DILUTION
If you invest in the ADSs, your interest will be diluted. After giving effect to
the issuance and sale of ordinary shares in the form of ADSs in this offering,
based on an assumed initial public offering price of US$6.00 per ADS, our as
adjusted net tangible book value would have been US$19.7 million, or US$0.84 per
ADS. This represents immediate dilution in net tangible book value of US$5.16 per
ADS to investors purchasing ADSs in this offering.

MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS OF FINANCIAL CONDITION AND RESULTS OF OPERATIONS
Results of Operations
Year Ended December 31, 2025 2024 S$ S$
Revenue 787,388 79,440
Cost of sales (642,562) (62,871)
Gross profit 144,826 16,569
Administrative expenses (3,682,794) (886,702)
Research and development expenses (1,843,294) (1,036,635)
Selling and marketing expenses (204,622) (49,186)
Finance expenses (176,883) (1,844)
Loss before income tax (5,445,573) (1,950,882)
Income tax expense - -
Loss after income tax and total comprehensive loss (5,445,573) (1,950,882)
Comparison of the Years Ended December 31, 2025 and 2024
Revenue Our revenue amounted to S$787,388 and S$79,440 in 2025 and 2024,
respectively, representing an increase of 891%. Gross profit amounted to S$144,826
and S$16,569 in 2025 and 2024, respectively. Gross profit margin decreased from
20.9% in 2024 to 18.4% in 2025.

Liquidity and Capital Resources
As of December 31, 2025 and December 31, 2024, we had cash at bank balances of
S$8,789,537 and S$959,226, respectively, and except for the amount due to MST
SingCo, we do not have any other loans or borrowings. The amount due to MST SingCo
is related to the 2021 License Agreement, under which consideration of S$2,880,000
was paid on our behalf directly by MST SingCo and we are obligated to repay that
amount.

INDUSTRY
The optical metalens industry includes participants in Singapore, Taiwan, the PRC,
Europe, and the United States. Industry market revenue is not MetaOptics company
revenue.

BUSINESS
We conduct operations through wholly-owned subsidiaries in Singapore and the
United States.

Customers and Suppliers
Haur-Jye Technology, Taiwan, was our largest customer and accounted for 74.6% of
our FY2025 revenue. MMI Systems Pte Ltd, Singapore, was our major supplier and
accounted for 81.0% of our FY2025 purchases. MMI Systems Pte Ltd is a wholly
owned subsidiary of MMI Holdings Limited, one of our principal shareholders.

License Agreements
We license key intellectual property from Accelerate Technologies Pte Ltd, the
commercialization arm of the Agency for Science, Technology and Research
("A*STAR"). The August 2023 License Agreement requires gross revenues of at least
S$3.0 million within five years from August 1, 2023. The December 2023 License
Agreement requires gross revenues of at least S$5.0 million within five years
from December 25, 2023. Some commercialization obligations had not been fulfilled,
but waivers were granted and the obligations were removed, amended, waived, or
extended.

Internal Control over Financial Reporting
We identified material weaknesses in our internal control over financial
reporting. Our policies and procedures did not comprehensively cover multiple
control areas, including segregation of duties, payroll, and purchases and
payables. We had insufficient accounting and financial reporting personnel with
IFRS knowledge, gaps in comprehensive accounting and reporting policies, and prior
corrections and restatements of previously issued financial statements.

PRINCIPAL SHAREHOLDERS
All directors and executive officers as a group hold 35.9% before this offering.
Principal shareholders include Angelling Capital Holdings Limited, MST SingCo,
and MMI Holdings Limited.

RELATED PARTY TRANSACTIONS
MMI Systems Pte Ltd is a wholly owned subsidiary of MMI Holdings Limited, one of
our principal shareholders, and accounted for 81.0% of our FY2025 purchases. The
amount due to MST SingCo is related to the 2021 License Agreement, under which
consideration of S$2,880,000 was paid on our behalf directly by MST SingCo and we
are obligated to repay that amount.

SHARES ELIGIBLE FOR FUTURE SALE
The company and certain directors, officers, and shareholders owning more than
50% of our ordinary shares prior to this offering have agreed to a 180-day
lock-up. SGX-ST Catalist moratorium restrictions remain in place until September
8, 2026 for our promoters, MST SingCo, Angelling Capital Holdings Limited,
indirect shareholders, pre-SGX IPO investors, and certain other shareholders.

DESCRIPTION OF AMERICAN DEPOSITARY SHARES
Our ordinary shares trade on SGX-ST Catalist under stock code 9MT and the ADSs
are proposed for Nasdaq trading under MOT. Each ADS represents 12 ordinary
shares. Holders may convert ordinary shares into ADSs and may surrender ADSs for
delivery of ordinary shares, subject to depositary procedures and applicable
market restrictions. Investors should reconcile the SGX ordinary share trading
price with the U.S. ADS price range using the 1 ADS to 12 ordinary share ratio.

UNDERWRITING
Roth Capital Partners, LLC and The Benchmark Company, LLC are acting as the
representatives of the underwriters. The underwriters have an option to purchase
up to an additional 15% of the ADSs sold in this offering, solely to cover
over-allotments, exercisable for 45 days.

Amount due to a shareholder 19 2,106,147 1,930,297 -

Going concern
The Group was in a net current asset position of S$8,550,435 as at December 31,
2025 and reported net loss of S$5,445,573 for the financial year ended December
31, 2025. The financial statements have been prepared on a going concern basis as
the directors are of the view that the Group has sufficient financial resources to
meet its financial obligations as and when they fall due in the next twelve months
period from the date of authorization of the consolidated financial statements
based on the cash flow projections prepared by management.
